meaning: adj.[生]生物适合的,不会引起排斥的
examples: Biocompatible ferrofluids normally use water as a carrier medium.
Conclusion The ceramic coating added by laser is testified in vitro to be a kind of good biocompatible ceramic.
Titanium has a lower modulus of elasticity-closer to that of host bone-and is more biocompatible than cobalt chrome.
What it amounts to is a series of 'smart' chips inserted into biocompatible devices and strung together.
Recently, researchers at the Instituto Italiano di Tecnologia (IIT) in Genoa created an artificial retina made of a biocompatible photovoltaic polymer.
